In [45]:
tech_name_SOL = ['backwards', 'brassy',  'aeolian-and-ordinario', 'near-the-board', 'slap-pitched', 'vibrato', 'bisbigliando',
 'harmonic-fingering', 'sul-tasto', 'multiphonics', 'tremolo', 'staccato', 'pizzicato-bartok', 'trill-major-second-up', 'sforzando',
 'glissando',  'flatterzunge']
print('%s types of playing techniques displayed: ' % (len(tech_name_SOL)))
print(*tech_name_SOL, sep = ", ")

17 types of playing techniques: 
backwards, brassy, aeolian-and-ordinario, near-the-board, slap-pitched, vibrato, bisbigliando, harmonic-fingering, sul-tasto, multiphonics, tremolo, staccato, pizzicato-bartok, trill-major-second-up, sforzando, glissando, flatterzunge
In [39]:
# find all .wav and .csv files in subdirectories
base_dir = '/import/c4dm-datasets/SOL_instrument/originalDataset/'
target = "*.wav"
wav_files = []   # create list

for path, subdirs, files in os.walk(base_dir):
    for name in files:
        if fnmatch(name, target):
            wav_files.append(os.path.join(path, name))   # add an element to the list
            
for k in range(len(wav_files)):
    wav_files[k] = wav_files[k].replace(base_dir, '')
In [46]:
instru_type = []

for jj in range(len(tech_name_SOL)):
    target_class = tech_name_SOL[jj]
    
    target_num = 0
    target_idx = []

    # if filename include target_class, count in
    for k in range(len(wav_files)):
        if target_class == wav_files[k].split('/')[2] and (wav_files[k].split('/')[1] not in instru_type):
            target_num += 1
            target_idx.append(k)
    if not target_idx:
        for k in range(len(wav_files)):
            if target_class == wav_files[k].split('/')[2]:
                target_num += 1
                target_idx.append(k)

    k = 0 # kth example

    targ_file = base_dir + wav_files[target_idx[k]]
    instru_type.append(wav_files[target_idx[k]].split('/')[1])
    sr, x = scipy.io.wavfile.read(targ_file)
    x = x.astype(float)
    D = librosa.amplitude_to_db(np.abs(librosa.stft(x)), ref=np.max)
    
    plt.subplots(nrows=1, ncols=2, figsize=(16, 4))
    y, sr = librosa.load(targ_file, sr=44100)
    ipd.Audio(y, rate=sr)
    
    plt.subplot(121)
    plt.plot(x)
    plt.title(wav_files[target_idx[k]].split('/')[1] + ': ' + target_class)

    plt.subplot(122)
    librosa.display.specshow(D, sr=sr, x_axis='time',  y_axis='log');  # plt.colorbar(format='%+2.0f dB')
    plt.title('Log-frequency power spectrogram')
    
    plt.show()
